zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnès de Condé de Morialmes於1215年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德孔代-素-利斯考特時，她的父親Nicolas de Condé45歲，母親Elisabeth de Morialmé26歲。她於1240年與Enguerrand de Fiennes結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1298年11月6日在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登享年83歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Condé
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Jorge, Ruben, Angel, Cesar, Fernando.  Portuguese Goncalo, Manoel.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from the title of rank conde ‘count’, a derivative of Latin comes, comitis ‘companion’. See Conte .
English (Shropshire): variant of Cundy , a cognate of 3 below, or Cound, a habitational name from Cound in Shropshire.

variants.name: Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Silvio, Silverio, Giuseppe, Sal, Alfonso, Biagio, Carlo, Emilio, Fernando, Gerardo.

Italian: from the title of rank conte ‘count’, from Latin comes, genitive comitis ‘companion’. Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .
Spanish: variant of   Conde   ‘count’ (see also 1 above).

variants.name: Candee
meanings: Americanized form of French Condé (see Conde ) or, alternatively, Candé, a habitational name from Candé in Maine-et-Loire, or from Candé-sur-Beauvron in Loire-et-Cher, or southern French Condy .
Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of English Condy , a cognate of the name Candé in 1 above.

History: The Candees trace their origin to Zaccheus Cande(e), who in the middle of the 17th century resided in the New Haven County, CT, and his probable father John Condy alias Conde. The name of the latter is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors as Jean/John Candée/Cande/Condé and in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America as John Conde. See also   Conde  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Agnès
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnès de Condé de Morialmes was born in 1215, in Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, her father, Nicolas de Condé, was 45 and her mother, Elisabeth de Morialmé, was 26. She married Enguerrand de Fiennes in 1240. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 6 November 1298, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, at the age of 83, and was buried in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnès de Condé de Morialmes est née en 1215, à Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Nicolas de Condé, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth de Morialmé, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Enguerrand de Fiennes en 1240. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 6 novembre 1298, à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterrée à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat 1240 Enguerrand de Fiennes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 6. ja.lifeSummary: Agnès de Condé de Morialmesが1215年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスノードコンドe-スル-ル'エスカウトで生まれた時，父Nicolas de Condéは45歳，母Elisabeth de Morialméは26歳であった。1240年にEnguerrand de Fiennesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1298年11月6日にイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンで83歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnès de Condé de Morialmes(이)가 1215년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 콘드e-수-'스카트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicolas de Condé(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elisabeth de Morialmé(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1240년 Enguerrand de Fiennes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1298년 11월 6일 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 사프런 월든에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnès de Condé de Morialmes родилась в 1215 г. в местности кондe-сур-л'ескаут, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Nicolas de Condé было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth de Morialmé, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Enguerrand de Fiennes в 1240 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 6 ноября 1298 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 83 лет и была похоронена в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Saffron Walden, Essex, England: Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnès de Condé de Morialmes nació en 1215, en Condé-sur-l'Escaut, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Nicolas de Condé, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Elisabeth de Morialmé, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Enguerrand de Fiennes en 1240. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 6 de noviembre de 1298, en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultada en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
